prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for pyridine were computed using the fukui-reactivity skill at the DFT level with the B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set (tier 'standard') in PySCF. The calculation was run in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1; the N+1 (anion) and N-1 (cation) states used multiplicity 2. Partial charges were obtained via Mulliken partitioning. Geometry was taken directly from the user-supplied pyridine.xyz without re-optimization at this level.

The most electrophilic site is atom 1 (N) with f+ = 0.48422572. The most nucleophilic heavy-atom site is atom 4 (C) with f- = 0.11826260. The dual descriptor Δf is most positive at N (+0.42010), confirming it as the dominant site for nucleophilic attack (electrophilic character), consistent with pyridine's well-known Lewis base behavior.

All integrity checks passed (sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f-) = 1.0000); integrity.trustworthy = true. No anomalous warnings. Provenance: computed this session.
